hilbertAnnihilatorRangeCriterionStatus.label: Hilbert annihilator range criterion
hilbertAnnihilatorRangeCriterionStatus.reason: For a densely defined closed trace map R between Hilbert spaces, closure Ran(R^*)=(ker R)^perp.  Hence E_active factors through the interval trace iff ker R is annihilated by E_active.

finiteActiveBlockFactorizationStatus.label: finite active block factorization
finiteActiveBlockFactorizationStatus.reason: Current Galerkin active blocks satisfy E_active=C_sample R_global to roundoff.  This is finite evidence for, not proof of, the continuum annihilator condition.

compactnessContradictionReductionStatus.label: compactness contradiction reduction
compactnessContradictionReductionStatus.reason: Failure of ker R subset ker E_active is equivalent to an A-normalized sequence with R f_n -> 0 and active source bounded below.  The high-block elliptic/Mosco theorem is the compactness mechanism needed to pass such a sequence to a continuum limit.

closedTraceActiveUniqueContinuationStatu.label: closed-trace active unique continuation
closedTraceActiveUniqueContinuationStatu.reason: Closed if the exact Green annihilation implication is available for every scalar active source row.  The remaining open part is endpoint/Fredholm compatibility in the continuum exact Green BVP.

continuumActiveTraceRangeCompatibilitySt.label: continuum active trace range compatibility
continuumActiveTraceRangeCompatibilitySt.reason: Open until the compactness/Mosco passage and the closed-trace active unique-continuation lemma are proved.

endpointFredholmCompatibilityFromActiveR.label: endpoint Fredholm compatibility from active range
endpointFredholmCompatibilityFromActiveR.reason: Once E_active lies in closure Ran(R^*), the endpoint vector b(d_u) annihilates ker(M^T), so b(d_u) lies in Range(M).  The endpoint Fredholm theorem then supplies z=-M^+b(d_u).

activeRangeEvidence.source: finite active block factorization E_active=C_sample R_global

frameContinuumPassageEvidence.conditionalConclusion: If the Galerkin active spaces converge in the A graph norm, trace quadrature converges uniformly on the limiting active space, and liminf gamma_N is bounded below by the observed positive frame floor (or any positive analytic lower bound), then the continuum observability inequality holds with that lower bound.

quadratureStabilityEvidence.interpretation: For bases with multiple trace counts, this measures stability of the weighted trace frame under trace mesh refinement.  This is a finite diagnostic for uniform quadrature convergence on the active subspaces.

ellipticTraceStatus.label: commuted Sturm/Volterra elliptic estimate
ellipticTraceStatus.reason: Prove the continuum source-range Hardy/Green estimate directly from the closed-trace Green identity and source-window coefficient bounds.

moscoLimsupStatus.label: Mosco limsup/recovery sequence
moscoLimsupStatus.reason: Requires A-graph dense Galerkin recovery inside the sampled closed-trace spaces with low/mid modes removed.

moscoLiminfStatus.label: Mosco liminf/compactness
moscoLiminfStatus.reason: Requires A-bounded Galerkin sequences with R_N f_N -> 0 to have closed-trace continuum limits after the high-block elliptic bound.

sourceOperatorNormConvergenceStatus.label: source operator norm convergence
sourceOperatorNormConvergenceStatus.reason: Formally follows once the direct source-row Green representers are constructed uniformly and the Galerkin spaces converge in the A graph norm.

volterraSturmLagrangeIdentityStatus.label: Volterra/Sturm Lagrange identity
volterraSturmLagrangeIdentityStatus.reason: Green's formula D_a B_P[K,f]=K Lambda_a(f)-f P^*K is verified symbolically for the moving trace operator.

distributionalInteriorGreenSourceStatus.label: distributional interior Green source
distributionalInteriorGreenSourceStatus.reason: The exact jump matrix represents each local source row as the singular part of P^*K at s0.

exactEndpointFredholmReductionStatus.label: exact endpoint Fredholm reduction
exactEndpointFredholmReductionStatus.reason: After the jump is fixed, the exact fundamental matrix gives P_delta Beta_z=M z+b(d), solvable iff b(d) lies in Range(M).

formalGreenAnnihilationImplicationStatus.label: formal Green annihilation implication
formalGreenAnnihilationImplicationStatus.reason: If the endpoint Fredholm compatibility is satisfied for an active source row, the resulting Green coefficient gives ell(f)=int_I K(a)Lambda_a(f) da, so closed trace implies ell(f)=0.

finiteOffBaseDerivativeRankUCStatus.label: finite off-base derivative-rank UC
finiteOffBaseDerivativeRankUCStatus.reason: Full rank of the off-base derivative response matrix proves the finite active unique-continuation implication on Galerkin active blocks.

sourceSideResponseNoncollapseStatus.label: source-side response noncollapse
sourceSideResponseNoncollapseStatus.reason: The Riesz-projector rank certificate proves noncollapse of the selected off-base derivative response on the certified source active plane.

endpointCompatibilityForActiveRowsStatus.label: endpoint compatibility for active source rows
endpointCompatibilityForActiveRowsStatus.reason: Closed either by rigorous full active row rank of M, which makes ker(M^*) trivial, or by combining the Green pairing formula with the Volterra/Sturm primal obstruction annihilation theorem.

closedTraceActiveUniqueContinuationStatu.label: closed-trace active unique continuation
closedTraceActiveUniqueContinuationStatu.reason: Closed if the exact Green annihilation implication is available for every scalar active source row.  The remaining open part is endpoint/Fredholm compatibility in the continuum exact Green BVP.

closedTraceActiveUniqueContinuationTheor.remainingAnalyticGap: Build a stable validated endpoint-flow solver that encloses the exact fundamental matrix Phi(a,s0) and endpoint rows with Frobenius endpoint-map radius below the rank margin.

endpointRangeTheorem.theoremName: exact adjoint Green endpoint range theorem

exactFundamentalMatrixEndpointMapStatus.label: exact fundamental-matrix endpoint map
exactFundamentalMatrixEndpointMapStatus.reason: For analytic coefficients with nonzero leading coefficient, P^*K=0 has a unique fundamental matrix Phi(a,t), and the endpoint concomitant is exactly Pi_delta Beta_z=Mz+b(d).

endpointFredholmRangeAlternativeStatus.label: endpoint Fredholm range alternative
endpointFredholmRangeAlternativeStatus.reason: The exact endpoint equation Mz=-b(d) is solvable iff b(d) is orthogonal to ker(M^T); the minimum-norm solution is z=-M^+b(d).

uniformTraceDualBoundFromCompactnessStat.label: uniform trace-dual bound from compactness
uniformTraceDualBoundFromCompactnessStat.reason: If rank M is constant, b(d_u) lies in Range(M), and d_u is continuous on the compact source window, then ||M^+b(d_u)|| and the trace-dual norm of the piecewise homogeneous Green solution are uniformly bounded.

finiteEndpointCompatibilityEvidenceStatu.label: finite endpoint compatibility evidence
finiteEndpointCompatibilityEvidenceStatu.reason: The sampled-flow diagnostic has rank one on the two active modes, but the actual endpoint right-hand sides lie in its range and are killed by the Moore-Penrose z choice.

continuumActiveRangeCompatibilityStatus.label: continuum active range compatibility
continuumActiveRangeCompatibilityStatus.reason: Open until the compactness/Mosco passage and the closed-trace active unique-continuation lemma are proved.

finiteEndpointEvidence.source: sampled-flow diagnostic, not the continuum definition

actualEndpointRhsInRange.label: actual endpoint RHS in Range(M)
actualEndpointRhsInRange.reason: For the actual source rows, the endpoint vectors b(d) are in the finite endpoint-map range.

activeEndpointKilled.label: active endpoint concomitant killed
activeEndpointKilled.reason: Minimum-norm z solves Mz=-b(d) on the active two-dimensional source space.

uniformTraceDualBoundClosed.label: uniform trace-dual bound
uniformTraceDualBoundClosed.reason: Still needs a rigorous continuum bound for the exact fundamental matrix and M^+ over the source window.

finiteActiveRangeEvidence.interpretation: Finite evidence for the continuum compatibility condition b(d_u) in Range(M).

hilbertAnnihilatorRangeCriterionStatus.label: Hilbert annihilator range criterion
hilbertAnnihilatorRangeCriterionStatus.reason: For a densely defined closed trace map R between Hilbert spaces, closure Ran(R^*)=(ker R)^perp.  Hence E_active factors through the interval trace iff ker R is annihilated by E_active.

finiteActiveBlockFactorizationStatus.label: finite active block factorization
finiteActiveBlockFactorizationStatus.reason: Current Galerkin active blocks satisfy E_active=C_sample R_global to roundoff.  This is finite evidence for, not proof of, the continuum annihilator condition.

compactnessContradictionReductionStatus.label: compactness contradiction reduction
compactnessContradictionReductionStatus.reason: Failure of ker R subset ker E_active is equivalent to an A-normalized sequence with R f_n -> 0 and active source bounded below.  The high-block elliptic/Mosco theorem is the compactness mechanism needed to pass such a sequence to a continuum limit.

closedTraceActiveUniqueContinuationStatu.label: closed-trace active unique continuation
closedTraceActiveUniqueContinuationStatu.reason: Closed if the exact Green annihilation implication is available for every scalar active source row.  The remaining open part is endpoint/Fredholm compatibility in the continuum exact Green BVP.

continuumActiveTraceRangeCompatibilitySt.label: continuum active trace range compatibility
continuumActiveTraceRangeCompatibilitySt.reason: Open until the compactness/Mosco passage and the closed-trace active unique-continuation lemma are proved.

definitions.compatibility: b(d) in Range(M), equivalently ker(M^T) annihilates b(d)
definitions.solution: z(d)=-M^+b(d) for the minimum-norm endpoint choice

traceDualBoundFormula: C_z <= ||M^+|| C_b on the compatible source family.
The endpoint trace-dual norm is bounded by the endpoint Sobolev trace constant times sup_a ||Y(a)|| and coefficient sup norms.
formalProof: Write P^*K=sum_{m=0}^8 c_m(a)K^(m) with c_8 nonzero on I. The first-order system Y'=A(a)Y has an exact fundamental matrix Phi by the standard linear ODE theorem.
The distributional source law fixes the jump Delta(d).  Thus the only remaining degrees of freedom are the eight entries of z=Y(s0-).
The Lagrange concomitant is bilinear in the endpoint jets of K and f.  After restricting f to the active source space and substituting the exact endpoint states, it becomes Mz+b(d).
Solvability of Mz=-b(d) is the finite-dimensional Fredholm alternative.  The Moore-Penrose solution gives the canonical choice and the sharp bound ||z||<=||M^+|| ||b(d)||.

For the actual source family d=d_u, continuity in u and compactness of the source window turn the pointwise bound into a uniform trace-dual bound, once the compatibility b(d_u) in Range(M) is supplied by the active trace range theorem.
endpointRangeTheorem.interpretation: The sampled ODE endpoint map has been demoted to finite evidence. The proof now uses the exact fundamental matrix and a precise range/compactness theorem.  The remaining global dependency is active trace range compatibility, not endpoint ODE sampling.

exactEquivalences: E_active in closure Ran(R^*) iff ker R subset ker E_active.
Failure iff there are f_n with ||f_n||_A=1, ||R f_n|| -> 0, and ||E_active f_n|| >= delta.
If compactness gives f_n -> f locally and R f_n -> 0, then Lambda_a(f)=0 on I.
The remaining unique-continuation lemma must show the same limit has E_active f=0, contradicting the lower active source bound.
formalProof: Let R:H_A->L^2(I) be the closed interval trace operator and let E_active:H_A->U_delta be the active source map.
By the closed-range/annihilator identity, a bounded active source row factors through R precisely when it vanishes on ker R.
If vanishing fails, normalize a sequence in the bad set.  The commuted Sturm elliptic estimate plus Mosco high-block exhaustion is the compactness engine producing a nonzero closed-trace limit.

The trace limit satisfies Lambda_a(f)=0 on the whole active interval.  The remaining analytic input is unique continuation in the Volterra/Sturm branch that forces the active source row of such a limit to vanish.

That contradiction gives ker R subset ker E_active.  Hence E_active is in closure Ran(R^*), giving the continuum trace-to-source representation and the endpoint Fredholm compatibility b(d_u) in Range(M).
remainingAnalyticGap: Build a stable validated endpoint-flow solver that encloses the exact fundamental matrix Phi(a,s0) and endpoint rows with Frobenius endpoint-map radius below the rank margin.
